basic_details.job_title: CSIR NIO Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Walkin for 27 Graduate, Technician Apprentice Posts
basic_details.short_information: CSIR-National Institute of Oceanography (NIO), Goa has invited candidates for 27 Apprentice Trainee posts under the Apprenticeship Act, 1961, through Notification No. NIO/HRM/Apprenticeship/01/2026 dated 18 June 2026. There is no written application process - eligible candidates must register on the NATS portal and directly attend a walk-in interview on 6 July 2026 at CSIR-NIO Headquarters, Dona Paula, Goa, with reporting time between 8:30 AM and 10:00 AM.

age_limit.details: Candidates asking about the age criteria for this apprenticeship can note that the limit differs for Technician and Graduate apprentices, calculated as on the date of interview. Technician/Diploma apprentices must be between 18 and 24 years , while Graduate/Degree apprentices must be between 21 and 26 years , both as on 6 July 2026. | Category Age Relaxation SC/ST 5 years OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years PwBD (General) 10 years PwBD (SC/ST) 15 years PwBD (OBC-NCL) 13 years Ex-Servicemen As per extant Government of India rules | Age relaxation is applicable only on production of a relevant certificate in the prescribed Government of India format, signed by the competent authority.

qualification.details: Candidates wondering whether they qualify for this apprenticeship can note that eligibility depends on the specific trade applied for, with separate qualification standards for Technician and Graduate apprentices. Both categories also share common conditions on passing year, prior apprenticeship history, and job experience that apply across all 27 posts. | Civil Engineering, Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Refrigeration and Air Conditioning Engineering, Computer Engineering: A three-year full-time Diploma in the relevant engineering discipline from a State Council/Board of Technical Education or recognized University/Institution. HR Management, Office Administration, Finance & Accounts, Stores & Purchase: Bachelor's degree in any discipline (B.A/B.B.A/B.Com/B.Sc or equivalent). R&D in Oceanography: Bachelor's degree in any science discipline such as Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Botany, Zoology, Biotechnology, Microbiology, Biochemistry, Environment Science, Marine Science, Geology, Computer Science, or IT. Library Sciences: Bachelor's Degree in Library and Information Science or equivalent from a UGC-recognized university. Dispensary/Nursing: B.Sc. in Nursing or equivalent. Guest House & Canteen Management: Bachelor's degree in Hotel Management or equivalent. Desktop Publishing: Bachelor's degree in Visual Arts, Photography, Videography, Visual Communication, or equivalent. | Beyond the academic qualification, CSIR-NIO has set additional conditions that apply uniformly to all applicants regardless of trade. Candidates who do not meet these conditions will not be considered, even if their educational qualification matches the post. | Only candidates who passed their qualifying exam in 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, or 2026 are eligible. Candidates must have registered on the Apprenticeship Portal (NATS) and fulfilled the eligibility criteria to appear for the walk-in interview. Candidates should not have undergone or be currently undergoing apprenticeship training in CSIR or elsewhere. Candidates should not have one year or more of job experience in any role. Sandwich diplomas, private diplomas, and part-time/distance/short-term/certificate courses are not acceptable. | Candidates asking about the age criteria for this apprenticeship can note that the limit differs for Technician and Graduate apprentices, calculated as on the date of interview. Technician/Diploma apprentices must be between 18 and 24 years, while Graduate/Degree apprentices must be between 21 and 26 years, both as on 6 July 2026. | Age relaxation is applicable only on production of a relevant certificate in the prescribed Government of India format, signed by the competent authority.

salary_details.details: Candidates asking how much CSIR-NIO apprentices are paid can note that the stipend is fixed by category and does not vary by individual trade within that category. Technician (Diploma) apprentices receive Rs. 10,900/- per month, while Graduate (Non-Engineering Degree) apprentices receive Rs. 12,300/- per month, both for a training period of 1 year. | On successful completion of training, apprentices will be awarded a National Apprenticeship Certificate by the National Council for Vocational Training (NCVT)/BOAT. CSIR-NIO has no obligation to offer regular employment after training, and apprentices cannot claim employment rights on the basis of completing the apprenticeship.

selection_process: Candidates curious about how CSIR-NIO will select apprentices can note that the primary mode is a direct walk-in interview
with a written test only triggered under specific conditions. Document verification happens at the interview itself
and selection is provisional until certificates are fully verified.

Step 1 - Document Verification: Original and self-attested documents are checked at the walk-in interview venue. Step 2 - Interview: Eligible candidates are interviewed by the Selection Committee starting at 10:15 AM. Conditional Written Test: If the number of candidates is higher than what can be reasonably interviewed

the Selection Committee may conduct a written examination and shortlist candidates for personal interview in proportion to available vacancies. Final Selection: Engagement is provisional and subject to subsequent verification of certificates and testimonials; false or suppressed information at any stage can lead to cancellation or termination even after engagement.
how_to_apply: Since this recruitment does not involve online form submission, candidates must directly attend the walk-in interview with a complete set of documents and a filled application form. Below is the step-by-step process candidates should follow.
Complete NATS registration and note the enrollment number.
Reach CSIR-NIO, Lok Bhavan Road, Dona Paula 403004, Goa (HRM Office) on 6 July 2026 between 8:30 AM and 10:00 AM.
Carry the filled application form along with all original certificates and two sets of self-attested photocopies.
Report to the Human Resource Management office for document verification and the interview process.
No TA/DA (travel allowance) will be provided for attending the interview, so candidates should plan their travel and stay independently.

_labels.notification_pdf: Download NIO Apprentices Official Notification PDF

Walk-In Interview (written exam only if candidates exceed proportion to vacancies)
CSIR-NIO, Lok Bhavan Road, Dona Paula 403004, Goa (HRM Office)

content_sections.heading: What Is the CSIR-NIO Apprenticeship Recruitment 2026?
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering what this recruitment involves can note that it is a one-year apprenticeship training opportunity, not a regular job, offered by CSIR-NIO under the Apprenticeship Act, 1961. The institute is seeking Technician (Diploma) and Graduate (Non-Engineering Degree) holders to undergo structured on-the-job training across engineering, administrative, and oceanography-related functions at its Goa headquarters. Selection happens entirely through a walk-in interview , with no separate online application form to fill before the interview date. Download NIO Apprentices Official Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: Important Dates for CSIR-NIO Apprenticeship 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates tracking the recruitment timeline should note that this notification has a single, fixed interview date rather than an application window. The notification was released on 18 June 2026 , and the walk-in interview is scheduled for 6 July 2026 , with candidates required to report between 8:30 AM and 10:00 AM; the interview itself begins at 10:15 AM for those who report on time. Candidates reporting after 10:00 AM will not be entertained under any circumstances, so arriving well within the reporting window is essential.

rows.date_time: 10:15 AM (for candidates who reported before 10:00 AM)

content_sections.heading: Vacancy Breakdown: 27 Apprentice Posts
content_sections.text: Candidates checking how many posts are open in each trade can refer to the tables below, which list all positions under both the Technician (Diploma) and Graduate (Non-Engineering) categories. The largest single category is HR Management, Office Administration, Finance & Accounts, Stores & Purchase with 10 posts , followed by R&D in Oceanography with 9 posts . All 27 posts carry a training period of 1 year , and reservation for SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/PwBD/EWS will follow Apprentices Act guidelines, provided candidates produce the appropriate category certificate at the interview; otherwise they will be treated as General category if eligible.

rows.trade: HR Management, Office Administration, Finance & Accounts, Stores & Purchase

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria for CSIR-NIO Apprenticeship 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering whether they qualify for this apprenticeship can note that eligibility depends on the specific trade applied for, with separate qualification standards for Technician and Graduate apprentices. Both categories also share common conditions on passing year, prior apprenticeship history, and job experience that apply across all 27 posts. Civil Engineering, Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Refrigeration and Air Conditioning Engineering, Computer Engineering: A three-year full-time Diploma in the relevant engineering discipline from a State Council/Board of Technical Education or recognized University/Institution. HR Management, Office Administration, Finance & Accounts, Stores & Purchase: Bachelor's degree in any discipline (B.A/B.B.A/B.Com/B.Sc or equivalent). R&D in Oceanography: Bachelor's degree in any science discipline such as Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Botany, Zoology, Biotechnology, Microbiology, Biochemistry, Environment Science, Marine Science, Geology, Computer Science, or IT. Library Sciences: Bachelor's Degree in Library and Information Science or equivalent from a UGC-recognized university. Dispensary/Nursing: B.Sc. in Nursing or equivalent. Guest House & Canteen Management: Bachelor's degree in Hotel Management or equivalent. Desktop Publishing: Bachelor's degree in Visual Arts, Photography, Videography, Visual Communication, or equivalent. Beyond the academic qualification, CSIR-NIO has set additional conditions that apply uniformly to all applicants regardless of trade. Candidates who do not meet these conditions will not be considered, even if their educational qualification matches the post. Only candidates who passed their qualifying exam in 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, or 2026 are eligible. Candidates must have registered on the Apprenticeship Portal (NATS) and fulfilled the eligibility criteria to appear for the walk-in interview. Candidates should not have undergone or be currently undergoing apprenticeship training in CSIR or elsewhere. Candidates should not have one year or more of job experience in any role. Sandwich diplomas, private diplomas, and part-time/distance/short-term/certificate courses are not acceptable. Candidates asking about the age criteria for this apprenticeship can note that the limit differs for Technician and Graduate apprentices, calculated as on the date of interview. Technician/Diploma apprentices must be between 18 and 24 years , while Graduate/Degree apprentices must be between 21 and 26 years , both as on 6 July 2026. Age relaxation is applicable only on production of a relevant certificate in the prescribed Government of India format, signed by the competent authority.

rows.age_relaxation: As per extant Government of India rules

content_sections.heading: Stipend and Training Period
content_sections.text: Candidates asking how much CSIR-NIO apprentices are paid can note that the stipend is fixed by category and does not vary by individual trade within that category. Technician (Diploma) apprentices receive Rs. 10,900/- per month , while Graduate (Non-Engineering Degree) apprentices receive Rs. 12,300/- per month , both for a training period of 1 year . On successful completion of training, apprentices will be awarded a National Apprenticeship Certificate by the National Council for Vocational Training (NCVT)/BOAT . CSIR-NIO has no obligation to offer regular employment after training, and apprentices cannot claim employment rights on the basis of completing the apprenticeship.

content_sections.heading: NATS Registration: A Mandatory First Step
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering how to begin the application process should know there is no conventional application form; instead, registration on the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS) portal is compulsory before attending the interview. Candidates appearing for the walk-in interview must register on the NATS portal and quote their enrollment number in the application form submitted at the interview. Visit https://nats.education.gov.in/ . Click on "Student - Student Register." Complete the registration form with accurate personal and educational details. A Unique Student Enrolment ID will be generated upon successful registration. Note down this enrollment number, as it is mandatory to mention in the application form at the walk-in interview. Candidates who have already registered on NATS in the past do not need to register again but must carry their existing enrollment number.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply: Walk-In Interview Process
content_sections.text: Since this recruitment does not involve online form submission, candidates must directly attend the walk-in interview with a complete set of documents and a filled application form. Below is the step-by-step process candidates should follow. Complete NATS registration and note the enrollment number. Download and fill the . Reach CSIR-NIO, Lok Bhavan Road, Dona Paula 403004, Goa (HRM Office) on 6 July 2026 between 8:30 AM and 10:00 AM . Carry the filled application form along with all original certificates and two sets of self-attested photocopies. Report to the Human Resource Management office for document verification and the interview process. No TA/DA (travel allowance) will be provided for attending the interview, so candidates should plan their travel and stay independently.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required at the Walk-In Interview
content_sections.text: Candidates preparing for the interview should carry a complete document set, since missing documents will lead to the candidature not being considered at all. CSIR-NIO requires both originals (for verification) and self-attested photocopies of all relevant testimonials. All certificates/testimonials related to educational qualifications, date of birth, and identity proof (e.g., SSLC/HSC/Birth Certificate). One passport-size photograph. Community/Category Certificate (for SC/ST/OBC-NCL/PwBD/EWS candidates, as applicable) in the prescribed Government of India format. Valid Aadhaar Card and Aadhaar number. NATS enrollment number/proof of registration. The duly filled application form along with mark sheets and relevant proof documents. Failure to submit any of the required documents at the time of the walk-in interview will result in the candidature not being considered , regardless of academic eligibility.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Candidates curious about how CSIR-NIO will select apprentices can note that the primary mode is a direct walk-in interview, with a written test only triggered under specific conditions. Document verification happens at the interview itself, and selection is provisional until certificates are fully verified. Step 1 - Document Verification: Original and self-attested documents are checked at the walk-in interview venue. Step 2 - Interview: Eligible candidates are interviewed by the Selection Committee starting at 10:15 AM. Conditional Written Test: If the number of candidates is higher than what can be reasonably interviewed, the Selection Committee may conduct a written examination and shortlist candidates for personal interview in proportion to available vacancies. Final Selection: Engagement is provisional and subject to subsequent verification of certificates and testimonials; false or suppressed information at any stage can lead to cancellation or termination even after engagement.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes Candidates Should Avoid
content_sections.text: Many candidates risk losing their interview opportunity due to avoidable preparation gaps rather than lack of qualification. Reviewing these common pitfalls in advance can help candidates attend the walk-in interview without last-minute issues. Skipping NATS registration before the interview: Since the enrollment number is mandatory on the application form, attending without completing NATS registration can lead to rejection at the venue itself. Arriving after the 10:00 AM reporting cut-off: CSIR-NIO has explicitly stated that candidates reporting after 10:00 AM will not be entertained, so late arrival means a wasted trip. Carrying incomplete document sets: Missing even one required certificate, such as the category certificate or Aadhaar card, can result in the candidature not being considered. Applying for engineering trades without engineering background or vice versa: Candidates appearing for trades other than those listed in the notification will not be entertained. Ignoring the passing year condition: Only candidates who passed their qualifying exam between 2022 and 2026 are eligible; older or pending qualifications make a candidate ineligible. Assuming TA/DA will be reimbursed: No travelling allowance is paid for attending the interview, so candidates should not expect reimbursement of travel expenses.

rows.link: CSIR-NIO, Lok Bhavan Road, Dona Paula 403004, Goa (Human Resource Management Office)

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies for Apprenticeship Training at CSIR NIO?
faq.answer: CSIR NIO has announced a total of 27 vacancies for Apprenticeship Training posts, including Technician and Graduate Apprentices.

faq.question: Q2. What is the application mode for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The application mode for this recruitment is Walk-in. Candidates need to attend the interview in person with all required documents.

faq.question: Q3. What are the minimum and maximum age limits for Technician Apprentices?
faq.answer: The minimum age limit for Technician Apprentices is 18 years, and the maximum age limit is 24 years.

faq.question: Q4. What are the educational qualifications required for Graduate Apprentices?
faq.answer: Graduate Apprentices need a Bachelor's degree in any discipline (Non-Engineering) or specific science disciplines for R&D roles, Library Sciences, Nursing, Hotel Management, or Visual Arts.

faq.question: Q5. What is the stipend offered to Technician Apprentices?
faq.answer: Technician Apprentices will receive a stipend of Rs. 10,900/- per month.

faq.question: Q6. What is the stipend offered to Graduate Apprentices?
faq.answer: Graduate Apprentices will receive a stipend of Rs. 12,300/- per month.

faq.question: Q7. Where is the Walk-in Interview for CSIR NIO Apprenticeship Training scheduled?
faq.answer: The Walk-in Interview is scheduled to be held at the CSIR NIO office in Dona Paula, Goa.

faq.question: Q8. What documents should candidates carry for the Walk-in Interview?
faq.answer: Candidates must carry all original testimonials, two sets of photocopies, a passport-size photograph, and a duly filled application form.

faq.question: Q9. Is there any age relaxation for reserved categories?
faq.answer: Yes, age relaxation is provided for SC/ST, OBC (NCL), PWD, and Ex-Servicemen categories as per government rules.

faq.question: Q10. What is the selection process for these apprenticeship roles?
faq.answer: The selection process primarily involves a Walk-in Interview. A written examination may be conducted if the number of candidates is high.
